$schema_note: PROVISIONING TEMPLATE for the Anthology Engine inbound intake route. provision-anthology-client.sh (W2.6) applies this into the client box's OpenClaw gateway config (top-level `hooks` object in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json) AS THE NODE USER, resolving every <CLIENT_*> slot per box. Credentials appear by LABEL only, never by value. The client-facing platform name is Convert and Flow. Nothing Anthropic ships in this file. Verified against the installed gateway (W0.4.json: OpenClaw 2026.6.11) live probe.
surface_note: Uses the built-in Gateway HTTP hooks surface (top-level `hooks:` config with a MAPPED hook), NOT the @openclaw/webhooks TaskFlow plugin. Rationale (W0.4.json): the intake payload is an ARBITRARY Convert and Flow form submission with hidden fields; the mapped-hook transform is the natural fit. The webhooks plugin forces a TaskFlow action contract ({action: create_flow, ...}) that a raw form submission is NOT, so it is REJECTED for intake.

per_client_secret_model.decision: SURFACE A (gateway-core mapped hook) with a BOX-SCOPED `hooks.token` bound by SecretRef to the label ANTHOLOGY_INTAKE_HOOK_SECRET. The box-wide token IS the per-client secret because each client runs their OWN single-owner OpenClaw box (PRD Section 15: the Command Center is per-client, single-owner, white-label, on the client's own box). Per-box == per-client isolation, at zero intake friction.
per_client_secret_model.why_not_per_route_secret: The gateway hooks surface on 2026.6.11 has NO per-mapping `secret` field (W0.4.json: normalizeHookMapping has no per-route secret); all /hooks routes share the ONE `hooks.token`. The only surface with a true per-route SecretRef is the webhooks plugin, which would force the TaskFlow action contract and break arbitrary intake. So per-route transport isolation is NOT available without breaking intake; per-box isolation is used instead.
per_client_secret_model.token_binding_note: SecretRef by LABEL; the value is NEVER inlined into config. `openclaw config get hooks` self-redacts the token ('__OPENCLAW_REDACTED__'), confirmed W0.4.json. The label resolves from the client env store or a 0600 secrets file (PRD Section 14).
per_client_secret_model.defense_in_depth: Because Surface A shares ONE box-wide token across ALL /hooks routes (the W0.4.json caveat: a box that ALSO runs other /hooks integrations shares the transport token), the anthology intake is isolated by TWO further mechanisms that do not depend on a per-route transport secret: (1) `match.source == 'anthology-intake'` so only Convert and Flow submissions carrying that source reach this transform; (2) intake_router.py performs its OWN independent route-secret check (SPEC 3.4 row 2 'secret check'; T2) against ANTHOLOGY_INTAKE_HOOK_SECRET, so intake auth does not rely on the gateway token ALONE and stays verifiable offline. On a dedicated client box this defense-in-depth is belt-and-suspenders; on a shared box it keeps anthology intake isolated.

hooks.path_note: Dedicated subpath; '/' is rejected by the gateway. Public reachability rides the named Cloudflare Tunnel -> loopback :18789/hooks/anthology-intake (NEVER Tailscale, per operator doctrine).
hooks.allowedAgentIds_note: Limits which agent a hook can target; resolved per box by the fleet installer to the client's anthology orchestrator agent id.

mappings.name: Anthology intake (Convert and Flow form submission -> deterministic S0 router)
mappings.match_note: path is the subpath after /hooks/. source matches payload.source so only tagged Convert and Flow submissions fire this mapping (isolation on a shared /hooks box).
mappings.action_note: OPEN MECHANISM (see cross_file_needs): the DETERMINISTIC transform module below converts the form payload and invokes intake_router.py with NO model call; the mapping action is a minimal/no-op acknowledge. W0.4.json observed only 'agent'|'wake' actions on 2026.6.11; W1.5/W2.6 must confirm the exact deterministic-dispatch wiring (transform-invokes-router vs a wake event consumed by a deterministic handler). Kept as the schema default 'agent' with deliver:false and allowUnsafeExternalContent:false so no model turn interprets untrusted form content as instructions.
mappings.deliver_note: Client-silent by doctrine: the hook never emits a client-facing message; only the three sanctioned nudge templates ever reach a client.
mappings.allowUnsafeExternalContent_note: Convert and Flow form data is UNTRUSTED external content; keep the gateway safety boundaries ON. Intake is deterministic (intake_router.py, no model call), so the payload is data, never instructions.
mappings.timeoutSeconds_note: Transport ceiling; intake_router.py itself acknowledges in under 2 seconds (SPEC 13.2 T4) and spawns the stage job detached.
mappings.transform_note: Deterministic transform module; lives under <configDir>/hooks/transforms/ (installed by provision-anthology-client.sh). Converts the Convert and Flow form payload into the intake_router.py dispatch. NO model call. Owns no secret; intake_router.py resolves ANTHOLOGY_INTAKE_HOOK_SECRET by label.

route_secret_label_note: MUST equal engine-config.template.json intake.route_secret_label; verify.sh / tests/test_webhook.py assert the two agree.
verifier_note: The nine intake proofs of SPEC 13.2 (T1..T9). Executed and OBSERVED on the canary at W5.3 AFTER provisioning; pre-provisioning it self-validates structure and reports the live battery as not-yet-executable.
provisioning_rule: provision-anthology-client.sh step 7 generates the route and its secret (stored under label ANTHOLOGY_INTAKE_HOOK_SECRET), writes this `hooks` block as the node user, installs the transform module under <configDir>/hooks/transforms/, refreshes the gateway (openclaw gateway restart or /new), then runs verify-webhook-t1-t9.sh. revoke-anthology-client.sh disables the mapping and probes the disabled route.
